name: Ballista Blast Expander
description: 4HP Ballista Blast modulation sidecar. Switchable LFO or envelope source, six destinations, clockable sync, and CV over depth, shape, and time with preset recall integration.

controls.name: Destination
controls.description: Chooses the Ballista Blast parameter target set.
controls.detail: harmonics · emphasis · parameter · pitch · amplitude · assignable

controls.name: Depth
controls.description: Sets modulation amount. Can behave as attenuator or attenuverter depending on configuration.

controls.name: Shape / Attack
controls.description: Shapes the LFO waveform or the envelope attack curve.

controls.name: Time / Decay
controls.description: Sets LFO rate or envelope decay time, depending on mode.

inputs.name: Clock / Trigger
inputs.description: Synchronizes or fires the modulation source.

inputs.name: Shape / Attack CV
inputs.description: CV over source shape or attack time.

inputs.name: Time / Decay CV
inputs.description: CV over source rate or decay time.

behaviors.name: Switchable source
behaviors.description: Acts as either an LFO or an envelope generator. The same expander can be clocked, triggered, or left free-running depending on the patch.

behaviors.name: Six destination routing
behaviors.description: The generated modulation can be aimed at harmonics, emphasis, pitch, amplitude, a generic parameter slot, or an assignable destination.

behaviors.name: Preset recall integration
behaviors.description: Depth, destination, and source behavior are stored with Ballista Blast presets, so the expander comes back with the sound.

behaviors.name: Dual-expander setup
behaviors.description: Two expanders can run in parallel on one Ballista Blast, letting one handle slow macro movement while the other adds short transients or random motion.

behaviors.name: Clocked modulation
behaviors.trigger: patch clock or trigger to the input
behaviors.description: The source can lock to the host patch, making rhythmic modulation sequences or envelope hits that line up with the voice.

patchIdeas.name: Envelope sidecar
patchIdeas.patch: Use the expander as a short envelope · route it to Ballista Blast pitch and amplitude · trigger with the same gate that opens the voice · gives plucked attacks without patching a second envelope generator.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ballista Blast Expander:
* Mode: envelope
* Destination: pitch + amplitude

Ballista Blast:
* Engine Select: FM

Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> Ballista Blast Expander (Clock / Trigger)
- Ballista Blast Expander (ENV) >> Ballista Blast (Pitch)
- Ballista Blast Expander (ENV) >> Ballista Blast (Amplitude)
- Ballista Blast (OUT L) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Slow harmonic drift
patchIdeas.patch: Free-running LFO · destination set to harmonics and emphasis · shallow depth · wavetable or classic engine stays in motion while the timbre slowly opens and closes around a stable note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ballista Blast Expander:
* Mode: LFO
* Destination: harmonics + emphasis
* Depth: low

Ballista Blast:
* Engine Select: Wavetable

LFO:
Output:

- LFO (Out) c> Ballista Blast Expander (Clock / Trigger)
- Ballista Blast Expander (LFO) >> Ballista Blast (Harmonics)
- Ballista Blast Expander (LFO) >> Ballista Blast (Emphasis)
- Ballista Blast (OUT R) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Randomized bite
patchIdeas.patch: Random source with syncable timing · destination assigned to pitch and parameter · one expander drives small pitch nudges while the other touches envelope depth, making each hit different without breaking the preset.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ballista Blast Expander:
* Mode: LFO
* Destination: pitch + parameter
* Shape / Attack: random

Ballista Blast:
* Engine Select: Classic

Clock:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Ballista Blast Expander (Clock / Trigger)
- Ballista Blast Expander (LFO) >> Ballista Blast (Pitch)
- Ballista Blast Expander (LFO) >> Ballista Blast (Parameter)
- Ballista Blast (OUT L) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual expander layering
patchIdeas.patch: One expander set to envelope, the other to LFO · the envelope drives amplitude while the LFO shifts harmonics · stored presets preserve both movements, so one patch can recall a whole performance scene.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ballista Blast Expander 1:
* Mode: envelope
* Destination: amplitude

Ballista Blast Expander 2:
* Mode: LFO
* Destination: harmonics

Ballista Blast:
* Engine Select: FM

Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> Ballista Blast Expander 1 (Clock / Trigger)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Ballista Blast Expander 2 (Clock / Trigger)
- Ballista Blast Expander 1 (ENV) >> Ballista Blast (Amplitude)
- Ballista Blast Expander 2 (LFO) >> Ballista Blast (Harmonics)
- Ballista Blast (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Ballista Blast (OUT R) -> Output (R)
